components.title: Multi-State Tour Compliance Worksheet
components.description: Generate a customized compliance checklist for touring between Illinois and Massachusetts

items: Prepare equipment inventory with serial numbers
Check registration status in each state
IL: Verify 6m height OR 2.5m lateral for unattended
MA: Verify 20ft height OR 8ft lateral for unattended
IL: Verify 3m height for attended Class 2+
MA: Verify 10ft height for attended Class 2+
Equipment specifications (mfg, model, class, power, wavelength)

components.title: Touring Show Compliance Challenge
components.description: Navigate a multi-state tour compliance scenario with real-world decision points

start.context: You're planning a 3-show tour: Chicago (IL), Springfield (IL), and Boston (MA). Show dates are 3 weeks away.

options.text: Submit advance notices for all three shows immediately
options.feedback: Good instinct, but let's verify the timing requirements.

options.text: Check current registration status in each state
options.feedback: Smart first step - registration status affects notification requirements.
Equipment compliance is important, but administrative requirements come first.

notice_timing.context: Both Illinois and Massachusetts require 10 working days advance notice. Your shows are 15, 18, and 21 days away.
notice_timing.decision: When should you submit each notice?

options.text: Submit all notices today to be safe
options.feedback: Excellent! Early submission ensures compliance and allows buffer time.

options.text: Submit IL notices now, wait for MA until 10 days before
options.feedback: Risky! Delays could result in late submission if issues arise.

registration_check.context: You discover your Massachusetts registration expires in 2 weeks.

options.text: Submit renewal immediately with 30+ days buffer
options.feedback: Good! MA allows grace period with early renewal.

options.text: Wait until after the Boston show to renew
options.feedback: Dangerous! Operating with expired registration violates regulations.

success_all_submitted.message: You've successfully navigated the compliance requirements for your multi-state tour! Both Illinois shows and the Massachusetts show have proper advance notice, and your early submission provides a safety buffer.

risk_delayed.message: While you may meet deadlines, delaying submissions increases compliance risk. Best practice is to submit all notices as early as possible.

renewal_submitted.message: Excellent decision! By submitting your MA renewal 30+ days early, you ensure continuous registration coverage for your Boston show.

risk_expired.message: This approach creates significant compliance risk. Massachusetts 105 CMR 121.027 lists 'operated after certificate expiration' as grounds for denial/revocation.
